clause.original: The customer receives a service credit where a material service outage exceeds the applicable tolerance period.
clause.revised: A customer-facing loss of a core service lasting more than four hours triggers a credit equal to 10% of the monthly fee. Each additional complete two hours adds 5%, capped at 20%. Provider system logs are primary evidence; customer operational records may rebut them.

expectations.customer: A material outage means serious loss of a core service for more than four hours.
expectations.provider: A material outage means complete platform unavailability for more than eight hours.

divergence.title: The same incident could produce opposite answers
divergence.summary: The parties disagree about both severity and duration. A six-hour loss of a core service would qualify for CustomerCo but not for CloudProvider under their original expectations.

protocol.trigger: Customer-facing loss of a core service lasting more than four hours
protocol.creditRule: 10% of the monthly fee, plus 5% for each additional complete two hours, capped at 20%
protocol.evidenceRule: Provider logs are primary; customer operational records may rebut them
protocol.authorityRule: AI may organise and propose; a human arbitrator decides disputed issues

clear.title: Complete six-hour record
facts: Core checkout service unavailable from 09:00 to 15:00
Provider logs cover the complete period
clear.explanation: The first four hours trigger 10%. One additional complete two-hour period adds 5%.

missing.title: Forty-five-minute log gap
facts: Provider logs are missing from 11:20 to 12:05
Customer operational records cover the missing period
missing.explanation: The evidence establishes more than four hours, but the additional complete two-hour period depends on contested evidence.

sources.title: Draft service agreement · clause 4.2
sources.excerpt: The customer receives a service credit where a material service outage exceeds the applicable tolerance period.

sources.title: CloudProvider incident log
sources.excerpt: Service interruption recorded 09:00–11:20 and 12:05–15:00. No log entries are available from 11:20 to 12:05.

sources.title: CustomerCo incident chronology
sources.excerpt: Core checkout functions were unavailable from 09:00 to 15:00. Support tickets continued throughout the provider-log gap.

dispute.headline: A six-hour service interruption is reported
dispute.summary: CustomerCo requests the contractual service credit. CloudProvider accepts the base trigger but disputes whether the missing log period supports the additional 5%.

caseMap.text: A customer-facing interruption occurred on 14 March.
The monthly service fee was CHF 10,000.
Whether the interruption is established for the complete six hours.
Apply 10% if more than four hours are established and add 5% only for a complete additional two hours.

inference.proposition: The qualifying interruption lasted six hours.
supportingEvidence: Support tickets during the missing period
Provider logs before and after the gap
contraryEvidence: CloudProvider disputes continuous unavailability during the gap
inference.status: AI inference · Partially supported · Human review required
inference.uncertainty: The forty-five-minute gap determines whether the additional 5% credit is available.

dispute.objection: The customer chronology assumes that all affected functions remained unavailable during the missing log period. That assumption is disputed.

review.preliminaryAssessment: The 10% base credit appears supported, but the additional 5% remains uncertain because the provider log has a forty-five-minute gap.

proposal.label: AI-proposed outcome · advisory only
proposal.summary: Count the reported six-hour interruption and apply a 15% service credit.
proposal.strongestCustomerPoint: Customer records and support tickets indicate continuous unavailability.
proposal.strongestProviderPoint: The primary evidence source has a gap during the period needed for the additional 5%.
proposal.assumption: The service remained unavailable throughout the missing provider-log period.

humanDecision.rationale: More than four hours are established, so the 10% base credit applies. The complete additional two-hour period is not established strongly enough for the extra 5%.
